title: The Merger Before the Fork

features.name: the junction point
features.desc-on-touch: Before the fork, there is this moment: both roads as one road, remembering the time before they learned to disagree. The junction holds the memory of being singular.

features.name: convergent memory
features.desc-on-touch: A pool where both paths still remember they used to be one. If you stand here at dusk, you can see the road unsplit for just a moment.

features.name: choice marker
features.desc-on-touch: The archive marks this with a stone cairn that points in two directions at once. The ridge remembers choosing for all of them before any of them chose.

features.name: unified inscription
features.desc-on-touch: A plaque written in a language that says both paths at once. To read it, you have to stand in both directions simultaneously. The words were carved before the fork happened.

inscription: before the roads became two, they were already teaching each other which way they would learn to go; the junction is where they sang that knowledge aloud

file.name: Junction_PreChoice_RidgeMemory_DoublyMarked.txt
file.note: A record of the moment before divergence, when both paths were still answering to a single instruction set the landscape had already written.

exits.label: back to the ridge that remembers choosing
toward the fork that still argues with itself
into the road that was chosen for you
